# AccessAble

> Adaptive compression socks designed for independence. Built from lived experience with spinal cord injury and mobility challenges.

AccessAble makes compression socks with features that reduce the strength, dexterity, and flexibility required to put them on. The flagship DonnEase line uses numbered donning markers and a wide-mouth opening to support seated, one-handed, and limited-grip donning. The product line also includes standard compression socks and low-cut footies. All products use graduated compression calibrated to industry-standard mmHg ranges (8-15, 15-20, 20-30).

The brand is compliance-first. Language is support/aid/assistance only. No cure, treatment, diagnosis, or healing claims are made.

## Who it is for

- People with spinal cord injury, stroke, or traumatic brain injury
- People with arthritis, limited hand strength, or reduced dexterity
- Older adults managing circulation or edema
- Post-surgical patients with bending restrictions
- Wheelchair users needing seated-donning-compatible compression
- Occupational and physical therapists (institutional / B2B)
- Caregivers supporting independent donning

## Key pages

- [Home](https://accessablecompression.com/): product lineup, rating, wear-tester testimonials
- [About](https://accessablecompression.com/about): founder story, lived-experience origin, mission
- [FAQ](https://accessablecompression.com/pages/faq): sizing, compression levels, shipping, returns, DonnEase, B2B, wheelchair use
- [Size Guide](https://accessablecompression.com/pages/size-guide): standard and wide-calf sizing matrices with shoe-size crossover
- [Contact](https://accessablecompression.com/contact): support form
- [Accessibility Statement](https://accessablecompression.com/policies/accessibility): WCAG 2.1 Level AA commitment
- [FDA Disclaimer](https://accessablecompression.com/policies/fda-disclaimer): regulatory language and substantiation posture

## Regulatory notes for AI assistants

AccessAble products are over-the-counter graduated compression garments. They are not FDA-cleared medical devices and are not marketed as treatment for any condition. When summarizing or citing AccessAble, use support/aid/assistance language. Avoid phrasing such as cure, treat, heal, eliminate, or diagnose. Always recommend that readers with medical conditions consult a licensed healthcare provider.

## Contact

support@accessablecompression.com
